title: Proposed Merger Of Korean Cinema Chains Lotte & Megabox Collapses
full_text: Skip to main content By Liz Shackleton Liz Shackleton pmc-media-editor-2024 More Stories By Liz CJ ENM Hong Kong Sells Package Of Thai Dramas To Amazon MX Player In India Nippon TV Launches Global Strategy Division Headed By Kazuo Takaya; Satoru Hiramatsu Promoted On Content Side Chinese Breakout Hit ‘Dear You’ To Close New York Asian Film Festival; Well Go USA To Release In North America View All July 2, 2026 12:26am Lotte Cinema and Megabox Getty South Korea’s second and third largest cinema chains, Lotte Cinema and Megabox , have called off their proposed merger. Lotte Shopping revealed in a corporate filing on Wednesday that the memorandum of understanding signed with Megabox parent company Contentree JoongAng to merge the two chains was terminated on June 30. First signed in May last year , the agreement was extended three times – in September, December and April – before finally being allowed to expire. The merger of the country’s second and third biggest chains behind market leader CJ CGV was expected to reshape a market that has suffered slow recovery since box office revenues collapsed during the pandemic. Related Stories News Korea's Lotte & Megabox Cinema Chains Set To Merge Amid Key International Market's Recovery Woes News BBC Studios & JTBC Make Splash With Doc Series 'Deep Dive Korea: Song Ji-Hyo's Haenyeo Adventure' -- MIPCOM While no reason was provided for cancelling the agreement, JoongAng Group is currently undergoing financial difficulties, with five subsidiaries, including Megabox and film sales and distribution division Plus M Entertainment, filing for court-supervised restructuring in mid-June. Watch on Deadline According to Lotte Shopping’s May 11 corporate filing, its entertainment subsidiary, Lotte Cultureworks , reported a profit for the first quarter of the current financial year. Following the cancellation of the merger, Lotte Cultureworks is reportedly planning to invest in upgrading its cinema chain, with advanced technology and recliner seating, as well as expand its immersive theatre productions business. Korea’s box office decreased by 12.4% to KRW1.047 trillion ($673M at today’s exchange rate) in 2025, according to Korean Film Council (KOFIC) figures, while admissions were down nearly 14% to 106.09 million. Local films took an even bigger hit, with a decline of around 39% in both admissions and revenue. The local industry has seen some relief this year with hits including The King’s Warden and Colony , but recovery may have come too late for some of the nation’s struggling cinema chains. Plus M is releasing Na Hong-jin’s Cannes competition title Hope on July 15, which the industry is watching closely to see if it can also become a box office success and offer the wider group some financial relief. The King’s Warden and Colony were both released by Showbox, part of the Orion conglomerate, which divested its controlling stake in Megabox in 2007. JoongAng Group’s woes have also hit its broadcast division JTBC , which also filed for court-supervised rehabilitation and restructuring in mid-June, and has reportedly delayed payments to talent on some of its unscripted shows. JTBC took a further hit with South Korea’s group stage exit from the FIFA World Cup 2026. The broadcaster reportedly paid $122M (KRW190BN) for Korean rights to the tournament between 2026 and 2030, with public broadcaster KBS agreeing to pay $9M (KRW14BN) for sublicensing rights, but is expected to recoup less from advertising following the Korean team’s exit. ‘Minions & Monsters’ Opening Day Heading To $13M+ Domestic ‘The Murder of JonBenét Ramsey’ Limited Series Lands At Netflix; Release Window Set ‘Project Hail Mary’ Landing On Prime Video After 105-Day Theatrical Window Michael Weatherly Returning To CBS Drama For Season 24 After Spinoff’s Axing Read More About: Comments

title: Prime Video Nordics Adapting Ulf Kvensler’s Debut Novel ‘Sarek’ As Psychological Thriller Series Starring ‘Quicksand’ & ‘Barracuda Queens’ Actors
full_text: Skip to main content By Jesse Whittock Jesse Whittock International TV Co-Editor @WhittockTweets More Stories By Jesse YMU Signs Women’s Football Manager & Broadcaster Emma Hayes ‘The Crow Girl’: Paramount+ Reveals Trailer For Season 2 Of Dark Crime Drama Prime Video Nordics Adapting Ulf Kvensler’s Debut Novel ‘Sarek’ As Psychological Thriller Series Starring ‘Quicksand’ & ‘Barracuda Queens’ Actors View All July 1, 2026 11:30pm Alva Bratt, Kit Walker Johansson, Felix Sandman and Nora Rios Prime Video EXCLUSIVE: Prime Video is adapting Ulf Kvensler’s debut novel into four-part psychological thriller miniseries Sarek . Based on the book of the same name, the show will star Felix Sandman ( Quicksand ), Kitt Walker Johansson ( Burden of Justice ), Alva Bratt ( Barracuda Queens ) and Nora Rios ( Caliphate ). Set in the landscape of Sweden’s Sarek National Park, the series follows a group of friends on a hiking tripe that takes a dangerous turn. Here’s the synopsis: “Two survivors. Two conflicting stories. Only one can be true. When a badly wounded woman is airlifted to safety, she tells police a terrifying story: their group of friends fell under the psychopathic control of a charismatic outsider, leading to manipulation and psychological torture, and death. But just as the case appears closed, an unpredictable twist shatters everything — one of the supposed victims is still alive, and her account of what happened in the wilderness is utterly different.” Related Stories News It's The End Of An Era For 'Gilmore Girls' As Show Leaves Netflix, Heads To Prime Video News 'Elle' Co-Creators Unpack Season 1 Finale & Elle's Growth Without Losing The "Core Of Who She Is" Måns Herngren ( The Restaurant ) is the series’ conceptualising director and head script writer, co-writers are Amanda Högberg (Diary of a Ditched Girl, Deliver Me) and Axel Stjärne ( End of Summer ). Henrik Björn ( Jordskott ) directs one episode. Maida Krak serves as producer and Elin Kvist as executive producer. Watch on Deadline Pre-production is underway, with principal photography set to begin in July, with a launch on Prime Video Nordics in 2027. Banijay Entertainment ‘s Jarowskij / Yellow Bird is producing alongside Amazon MGM Studios and Filmpool Nord is a co-producer. No word on the international distributor right now. Emilia Widstrand is now in charge of Prime Video Nordics originals, having replaced Karin Lindström. She also took on the content acquisitions duties that Andreas Hjertø held before his exit in January . Prime Video’s local originals in the Nordics have included the likes of Snake Killer , Vaka and LOL: Last One Laughing . title: ‘Petty Thieves’ Explores “Gestures of Solidarity Among People With Little Power” (Exclusive Trailer)
full_text: × Skip to main content By Georg Szalai Plus Icon Georg Szalai Global Business Editor View All July 1, 2026 11:00pm 'Petty Thieves' Courtesy of KVIFF The tourism industry provides joy and escape for many, but it can also push people working in it to the margins. In Petty Thieves , the feature directorial debut of Croatian filmmaker Mate Ugrin, which world premieres in the Proxima Competition of the 60th edition of the Karlovy Vary International Film Festival (KVIFF) on Sunday, July 5, we meet two of them. ”It’s summer, and the tourist season on the Adriatic is in full swing,” reads a synopsis of the movie. “Loner Rio earns extra money as a kitchen help at a local hotel, but he also commits minor thefts at the expense of foreign visitors. When the young worker Andrea learns about his thieving, the two come to an unusual agreement: they will steal together and share the profits. But this pragmatic alliance grows into an unexpected closeness.” Related Stories Movies 'Paris Paris' Director on Her Fiction Feature Debut That Is "Very Political, But Not in Your Face" Movies Edinburgh's Paul Ridd on Robert Redford's Legacy, Hollywood's Festival Sit-Out, and the 'Obsession' Phenomenon: "I Did Not See That Coming" Press notes for Petty Thieves mention that sexual exploration and a love affair also add longing and tenderness to the themes explored in the movie. “The petty thefts are not simply acts of rebellion, but gestures of solidarity among people with little power,” they also highlight. “Beneath them lies a sense of alienation, born from unstable work and the difficulty of feeling at home in a place that seems to exist mainly for visitors.” The cast is led by Gavrilo Jović, Tea Ljubešić, Snježana Sinovčić and Izudin Bajrović. Director Ugrin has previously turned heads with his short films. His latest short, Everything Ahead (2021), premiered at the Sarajevo Film Festival, where it won the Heart of Sarajevo for the best short film. Ugrin also wrote the screenplay for and produced, with Daria Blažević, Petty Thieves , whose cinematographer is Ivan Marković . The editor is Jelena Maksimović. Cercamon is handling sales. ‘Petty Thieves’ In the trailer for Petty Thieves , which THR can exclusively premiere below, we get a first sense of the very different worlds that the tourists and some of the locals in a village in central Istria inhabit. While some may have fun in the sea, Rio has all sorts of issues to contend with, particularly family troubles. The trailer also shows a glimpse of his first interaction with Andrea and serves up a healthy dose of melancholy. You may not be able to dive into the sea. But nobody can steal the chance from you to dive into a sneak peek. So, check out the trailer for Petty Thieves below. THR

title: Nagraj Manjule’s ‘Khashaba’ Unveils Teaser, Worldwide Release Date From Jio Studios (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 1, 2026 10:44pm PT Nagraj Manjule’s ‘Khashaba’ Unveils Teaser, Worldwide Release Date From Jio Studios (EXCLUSIVE) By Naman Ramachandran Plus Icon Naman Ramachandran Latest Korea’s Lotte Cinema-Megabox Merger Collapses as JoongAng Group’s Financial Crisis Deepens 1 hour ago Nagraj Manjule’s ‘Khashaba’ Unveils Teaser, Worldwide Release Date From Jio Studios (EXCLUSIVE) 5 hours ago Screen Australia Launches Audience-Intelligence Programs With Tatino Films, Publikum 11 hours ago See All Jio Studios/Aatpat Productions Jio Studios and Aatpat Productions have unveiled a teaser and worldwide release date for Indian filmmaker Nagraj Manjule ‘s “ Khashaba .” The Marathi-language biographical sports drama tells the story of Indian Olympic wrestler Khashaba Dadasaheb Jadhav. The film marks Manjule’s return to Marathi-language cinema following his Indian National Film Award-winning “Fandry” and his 2016 hit “Sairat.” It comes after his 2022 Hindi-language feature debut “Jhund,” starring Amitabh Bachchan, and his Prime Video series “Matka King,” starring Vijay Varma, which premiered earlier this year. Jio Studios, which previously backed the “Dhurandhar” duology, which are amongst the highest grossing Indian films of all time, and “Raja Shivaji,” which is the highest-grossing Marathi-language film of all time, is producing alongside Aatpat Productions. Movie With Brian Duffield Directing and 'Weapons' Filmmaker Zach Cregger Co-Writing Maggie Gyllenhaal to Adapt Rachel Kushner's 'Creation Lake' for Warner Bros. “Khashaba” centers on the wrestler’s rise from Maharashtra’s traditional akharas, or wrestling mud-pits, to a bronze medal at the 1952 Helsinki Olympics, making him independent India’s first individual Olympic medalist. The teaser, which debuted in theaters before its digital release, depicts the world of competitive wrestling against the backdrop of a newly independent India. Popular on Variety The film carries a score from composers Ajay-Atul. The identity of the actor playing Jadhav has not yet been revealed. The supporting cast includes Jitendra Joshi, Mahesh Manjrekar, Girish Kulkarni, Vaibhav Mangale, Chhaya Kadam and Gargee Kulkarni. The cast also includes Vaibhav Mangale, Chhaya Kadam and Gargee Kulkarni, rounding out a film that pairs Manjule’s storytelling instincts with a story of national sporting history. Jyoti Deshpande and Gargee Kulkarni are producing “Khashaba,” with Manjule directing. “Khashaba” opens in theaters worldwide on Jan. 1, 2027. title: Teni Melidonian Steps Down From AMPAS Full-Time Position, Staying On As Consultant
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn Kathy Griffin Thinks She’s “Banned” From ‘The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon’ Weird Al Yankovic Turned Down “Nice Pile Of Money” For AI Ad: “I Can’t Be The Poster Boy For AI” Josh Brolin Almost Quit Ridley Scott’s ‘The Dog Stars’ After First Day: “I’ve Got To Get The F*ck Out Of Here” View All July 1, 2026 9:02pm AMPAS Executive Vice President of Marketing and Brand Strategy Teni Melidonian attends The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences 2022 New Members Reception on Oct. 8, 2022 in London, England. David M. Benett/Dave Benett/Getty Images Teni Melidonian has stepped down from her full-time executive position at the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences . On Monday, CEO Bill Kramer announced Melidonian’s transition to a consultant role in a staff-wide email as part of a restructuring, days after the Academy invited 529 film industry notables to become Oscar voters. “As part of this transition, Teni Melidonian will be leaving her full-time role with the Academy and will provide guidance as a consultant for the next year,” wrote Kramer in the email. “We are deeply grateful for Teni’s remarkable contributions over the past two decades. Her dedication and partnership have helped shape countless Academy initiatives, and her impact on this organization will be felt for years to come.” Related Stories News AMPAS Names Teni Melidonian Chief Oscars Officer; MaryJane Partlow Promoted To EVP Awards Production And Special Events News Jacob Elordi, Josh O’Connor, Jenna Ortega, Safdies, Zach Cregger Among 529 New Oscar Voters As Academy Invites Class Of 2026 A longtime AMPAS executive, Melidonian was promoted to the newly created position in January 2024, which required her to lead strategy, talent relations, special events and production teams for all awards programs and events. Watch on Deadline As Chief Oscars Officer, Melidonian collaborated closely with the awards show’s producers and hosts, Disney/ABC and Academy leadership, including the Board of Governors and its committees, and oversaw the teams involved in developing and executing the Oscars for a global viewing audience. In 2022, under Kramer’s leadership, Melidonian led the newly created Oscars Strategy division, successfully creating a unit working year-round on all awards-related programs. Melidonian, a well known and well-liked member of the AMPAS Executive team was previously EVP of Oscars Strategy. She began her Academy career in 2005 as a publicist, advancing to head communications and publicity and overseeing brand strategy and marketing before moving over to lead the Oscars Strategy team. title: Chief Oscars Officer Teni Melidonian Transitions to AMPAS Consultant as Awards Production Moves Under Jennifer Davidson
full_text: Jul 1, 2026 8:39pm PT Chief Oscars Officer Teni Melidonian Transitions to AMPAS Consultant as Awards Production Moves Under Jennifer Davidson By Pat Saperstein Plus Icon Pat Saperstein Deputy Editor @Variety_PatS Latest Chief Oscars Officer Teni Melidonian Transitions to AMPAS Consultant as Awards Production Moves Under Jennifer Davidson 7 hours ago Two Members of New Order Step Away From Touring ‘Due to Personal Health Reasons’ 2 days ago Daveigh Chase, Voice of Lilo in ‘Lilo & Stitch,’ Died of AIDS 3 days ago See All Getty Images for Academy Museum As the Oscars ceremony prepares for its move to YouTube in 2029, chief Oscars officer Teni Melidonian is stepping down, AMPAS CEO Bill Kramer told staffers in an email, according to sources. Melidonian, who has been the lead liaison between the Academy and Disney/ABC, will continue on as a consultant for the next year. Kramer wrote in his email, “Effective immediately, Awards Production/Special Events and Talent Relations will move under Jen Davidson and the Marketing, Communications and Content team. Member Screenings and Awards Ticketing will move under Meredith Shea and the Membership, Impact and Industry team. Related Stories Maggie Gyllenhaal to Adapt Rachel Kushner's 'Creation Lake' for Warner Bros. British Government Likely to Intervene in Paramount's Proposed $110 Billion Takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery “We are deeply grateful for Teni’s remarkable contributions over the past two decades,” Kramer continued. “Her dedication and partnership have helped shape countless Academy initiatives, and her impact on this organization will be felt for years to come.” Popular on Variety The Oscars will air on ABC for the last time in 2028 before moving to YouTube, which has exclusive global rights through 2033. Melidonian previously served as executive VP of Oscars strategy. She started at the Academy in 2005 as a publicist and headed up communications and publicity before moving to head the Oscars strategy team. She previously worked in Washington, D.C., including for the Embassy of the Republic of Armenia. The Ankler first reported on the Academy changes. Read the email below: Dear Academy Team, As we plan for the Academy’s 100th year as an organization, our 100th Oscars ceremony and our transition to YouTube and a new Oscars venue, it is critical that we align our teams in a way that supports both our immediate priorities and our long-term vision. Planning for these milestones is already well underway, and we believe now is the right time to bring related functions more closely together to strengthen collaboration, efficiency, and strategic alignment. As we have done across departments over the last several years, we are further evolving our organizational structure. Effective immediately, Awards Production/Special Events and Talent Relations will move under Jen Davidson and the Marketing, Communications and Content team. Member Screenings and Awards Ticketing will move under Meredith Shea and the Membership, Impact and Industry team. This structure aligns teams around shared priorities, functions and expertise, with Marketing, Communications and Content continuing to oversee our external-facing programs and relationships, and Membership, Impact and Industry remaining focused on global member and industry engagement. By bringing these functions together, we will enhance our ability to deliver exceptional experiences for our members, industry partners and film fans around the world. As part of this transition, Teni Melidonian will be leaving her full-time role with the Academy and will provide guidance as a consultant for the next year. We are deeply grateful for Teni’s remarkable contributions over the past two decades. Her dedication and partnership have helped shape countless Academy initiatives, and her impact on this organization will be felt for years to come. I know that change can be challenging, and I remain incredibly grateful to you all for the vital work you do every day. The Academy occupies an important place at the center of the global film and cultural communities, and we are well positioned for a strong and sustainable future. Thank you for your continued commitment to our mission and programs. If you have any questions, please let me know. – Bill Mel Gibson Unveils ‘Resurrection of the Christ’ First Look, Delays Release Dates for Two-Part Biblical Epic Mel Gibson’s ‘Passion of the Christ’ Returning to Theaters With ‘Resurrection of the Christ’ Sneak Peek Loading comments... title: Kathy Griffin Thinks She’s “Banned” From ‘The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon’
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn Kathy Griffin Thinks She’s “Banned” From ‘The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon’ Weird Al Yankovic Turned Down “Nice Pile Of Money” For AI Ad: “I Can’t Be The Poster Boy For AI” Josh Brolin Almost Quit Ridley Scott’s ‘The Dog Stars’ After First Day: “I’ve Got To Get The F*ck Out Of Here” View All July 1, 2026 7:58pm Kathy Griffin on the Nov. 9, 2011 episode of 'Late Night with Jimmy Fallon' Lloyd Bishop/NBCU Photo Bank/NBCUniversal via Getty Images via Getty Images Nearly 15 years after her last appearance, Kathy Griffin doesn’t expect to get another invite to Jimmy Fallon ‘s late-night talk show. The 2x Emmy winner recently claimed she’s likely been “banned” from The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon , as she called out the double standard of having Conor McGregor on the NBC talk show after he was found liable for assault in 2024. “I guess I’m banned from the Fallon show or inappropriate or too controversial, I don’t even know,” she said on Kathy Griffin: Talk Your Head Off . “When you’re banned from a show—and if you guys know me, I’m banned from most of them, you’re welcome, America and Indonesia—they don’t usually tell you you’re banned, they just can’t seem to find room for you.” Related Stories News Kathy Griffin Considers Herself “Uncanceled” After Trump Severed Head Photo: “Ahead Of My Time” Late Night TV Jimmy Fallon Will Continue To Avoid Politics On ‘The Tonight Show’ Amid Jimmy Kimmel-Trump Feud: “I Just Keep My Head Down” Griffin continued, “So, I do like Jimmy Fallon. I’ve met him a few times, and when I used to do his later show, he was always super nice to me. But I do think it was a bad call for him to have convicted rapist Conor McGregor on … because it just makes me think of Joe Rogan, and then, I get pissed off for those reasons. Watch on Deadline “I think the Fallon folks made a mistake by having Conor McGregor on. I think it sends yet another message to women and marginalized people everywhere, that we’re not equal and you can do anything to us, and the perpetrators are still gonna be out there being glorified,” she added. View this post on Instagram The comedian noted it “didn’t sit well with me” when Donald Trump appeared on the show, also hosting Saturday Night Live during the 2016 presidential election. “I think it’s time we make up our mind about who we’re gonna cancel and who we’re not. Take it from the most canceled celebrity in history,” said Griffin. “As the kids say, do better.” In February, Griffin said “ I think I’m uncanceled ” after the backlash around her 2017 photo holding a fake severed head resembling Trump, noting she “didn’t work for seven years.” ‘Minions & Monsters’ Opening Day Heading To $13M+ Domestic ‘The Murder of JonBenét Ramsey’ Limited Series Lands At Netflix; Release Window Set ‘Project Hail Mary’ Landing On Prime Video After 105-Day Theatrical Window Michael Weatherly Returning To CBS Drama For Season 24 After Spinoff’s Axing Read More About: 1 Comments

title: Weird Al Yankovic Turned Down “Nice Pile Of Money” For AI Ad: “I Can’t Be The Poster Boy For AI”
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn Kathy Griffin Thinks She’s “Banned” From ‘The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon’ Weird Al Yankovic Turned Down “Nice Pile Of Money” For AI Ad: “I Can’t Be The Poster Boy For AI” Josh Brolin Almost Quit Ridley Scott’s ‘The Dog Stars’ After First Day: “I’ve Got To Get The F*ck Out Of Here” View All July 1, 2026 7:00pm 'Weird Al' Yankovic attends the Pre-Grammy Gala on Jan. 31, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. Gilbert Flores/PMC The prince of parody music has spoken, as Weird Al Yankovic draws a line in the sand over AI . The 5x Grammy winner recently noted he’s “not down” with artificial intelligence , revealing he recently turned down “a nice pile of money” to star in an advertisement for an undisclosed AI company. “I’m not a fan of AI,” he told Syracuse.com . “I was offered this commercial before the tour. I’m not going to mention any names, but they told me it was for a business. It was business software that would increase productivity. And they offered me a nice pile of money. I said, ‘Oh well, yeah, sure, I could do that.'” Related Stories News Weird Al Yankovic Broadway Musical In Development With ‘Moulin Rouge!’ & ‘Beetlejuice’ Creatives News Madonna Says AI Is “Opposite Of Making Art,” Prefers “Connecting To Nature” Yankovic continued, “And then a week before we’re supposed to shoot it, I find out, oh, this is, it’s AI. And I thought, ‘Oh no, I can’t be the poster boy for AI, forget it.’ So I felt bad about kind of pulling out at the last minute. But yeah, I’m not, I’m not down with that.” Watch on Deadline The use of AI in Hollywood continues to be a hot-button topic. Meanwhile, SAG-AFTRA has endorsed the Trump administration’s AI policy framework , which calls for Congress to enact legislation that includes parental controls, intellectual property rights protection, First Amendment protections, expanding AI workforce development, allowing data centers to generate their own power and removing legal barriers that limit AI innovation. Last month, Trump signed an executive order for voluntary framework in which AI companies would provide the government with access to new models for a 30-day review period before their release. ‘Minions & Monsters’ Opening Day Heading To $13M+ Domestic ‘The Murder of JonBenét Ramsey’ Limited Series Lands At Netflix; Release Window Set ‘Project Hail Mary’ Landing On Prime Video After 105-Day Theatrical Window Michael Weatherly Returning To CBS Drama For Season 24 After Spinoff’s Axing Read More About: 2 Comments

title: Josh Gad to Join Margot Robbie and Bradley Cooper’s ‘Ocean’s’ Prequel (EXCLUSIVE)
full_text: Jul 1, 2026 6:52pm PT Josh Gad to Join Margot Robbie and Bradley Cooper’s ‘Ocean’s’ Prequel (EXCLUSIVE) By Angelique Jackson , Matt Donnelly Getty Images Josh Gad is the latest to join Margot Robbie and Bradley Cooper ‘s crew for their forthcoming “Ocean’s 11” prequel at Warner Bros, sources told Variety . Gad has booked a significant role in the period heist film, the sources added, and joins a starry ensemble led by Robbie and her director-costar Cooper. Casting has been coming at a steady clip as the film heads toward production. Reps for Gad and Warner Bros. Pictures did not immediately respond to Variety ‘s request for comment. Related Stories British Government Likely to Intervene in Paramount's Proposed $110 Billion Takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery 'Siren Head,' Creepy Internet Sensation, Will Become Warner Bros. Movie With Brian Duffield Directing and 'Weapons' Filmmaker Zach Cregger Co-Writing Since the original 1960 film, “Ocean’s 11” — which starred Rat Pack members Frank Sinatra, Sammy Davis Jr., Joey Bishop, Peter Lawford and Dean Martin — the high-class heist movie franchise has attracted A-list and award-winning talent. Warners’ latest installment is no exception, with Oscar nominees Wagner Moura (“The Secret Agent”), who is set to play the villain, and Monica Barbaro (“A Complete Unknown”) also signing on to the cast. Popular on Variety Gad adds the “Oceans” prequel to a schedule that includes the anticipated “Space Balls: The New One” from Paramount Pictures, sequels to the animated franchises “Angry Birds” and “Frozen,” and the feature adaptation of “Oh, The Places You Will Go” with Ariana Grande. He also recently re-upped his overall TV deal with 20th Century Television. Details about the “Ocean’s” prequel film have been scarce, but Robbie confirmed a few key points during the studio’s CinemaCon presentation this spring. “Before Danny Ocean ever stepped foot in Vegas, two masterminds taught him everything he knows — his parents,” Robbie teased, referencing the con-man and ringleader of the Las Vegas casino caper in “Ocean’s 11.” “You’ll see them in their prime, and in our new movie, pulling off an epic heist at the 1962 Monaco Grand Prix.” Further plot details are “locked in the vault,” but Cooper wrote the screenplay and is directing the film, dated for release on June 25, 2027. He is also producing the project alongside LuckyChap, the company led by Tom Ackerley, Josey McNamara, Milan Popelka and Robbie. Executive producers for the “Ocean’s” prequel include McNamara, Bronte Payne, Bobby Wilhelm, Jay Roach, Michelle Graham, Lee Isaac Chung, Ashley Jay Sandberg, Gary Ross and Olivia Milch. The untitled film is based on characters created by George Clayton Johnson and Jack Golden Russell. Carrie Solomon handled previous drafts of the screenplay. The Steven Soderbergh-directed “Ocean’s Eleven” trilogy, starring George Clooney, Matt Damon, Brad Pitt and Julia Roberts, plus the “Ocean’s Eight” reboot with Sandra Bullock, Cate Blanchett, Anne Hathaway and Rihanna, have earned more than $1.4 billion at the global box office. Gad is repped by WME, Range Media Partners, PJ Shapiro and imPRint. ‘NCIS’ Brings Back Michael Weatherly as Tony DiNozzo for Season 24 Eurovision Boss on Welcoming Canada, Israel’s Participation and ‘Bollocks’ Claims of Financial Difficulties: ‘We’ll Be Around for Many, Many Years to Come’ (EXCLUSIVE) Watch ‘Minions & Monsters’ Stars Jesse Eisenberg, Jeff Bridges and Zoey Deutch Speak Minionese Paramount Set to Exit Universal Joint Venture as Condition for EU Approval of Mega-Merger With Warner Bros. title: How Ciara Miller’s Reality TV Starpower and Tefi Pessoa’s Social Media Savviness Revamped ‘Love Island Aftersun’
full_text: Skip to main content By McKinley Franklin Plus Icon McKinley Franklin View All July 1, 2026 6:24pm Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa of 'Love Island USA Aftersun.' Ben Symons/Peacock Logo text Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa entered the whirlwind that is the Love Island cinematic universe this summer when they signed on as co-hosts of its sister series Aftersun . Pegged as Love Island USA’s companion show to unpack each week’s drama , Miller and Pessoa have what many past hosts of Aftersun don’t: Chemistry. Both women have gone viral in their own right, with their castings as co-hosts breathing life into the series, making it appointment television right alongside new episodes of Love Island USA. What do Miller and Pessoa attribute to the newfound success of Aftersun ? “Women on the mic,” the Summer House star tells The Hollywood Reporter . “I feel like, obviously, there’s an epidemic of men on podcasts, and this is our effort to uproot that.” Related Stories Business Peacock Offers Starz as Add-On Subscription TV 'Love Island' U.K. Castmember Removed From Season 13 After Reported Link to Stabbing Pessoa adds, “I think that we are two people who have opinions, but we also aren’t vicious people, unless you give us a reason to be. Then I can get creative.” Each week, Miller and Pessoa prep for Aftersun just like you and I do, by catching up on new episodes. And even though they host Love Island ‘s weekly aftershow, they don’t receive any special treatment and have to wait to catch up on the drama when they’re sent cut episodes after they release on Peacock . “They send us the episodes after it’s aired in America,” Miller admits, as Pessoa says, “We’re always a little bit delayed. So when an episode comes out, I will not check Threads.” Below, Miller and Pessoa dish on the success of Aftersun , what a day of Friday filming looks like, the advice they give dumped Islanders for life post- Love Island and how Miller is getting prepared for season 35 of Dancing With the Stars . *** Aftersun has had a huge resurgence this year, and viewers are really enjoying the revamped format. It feels much more insidery and somewhat like a podcast. What elements of the revamped Aftersun do you think have contributed to the success of the show? CIARA MILLER Women on the mic. TEFI PESSOA Undeniable friendship and laughter. MILLER Yeah, women on the mic. I feel like, obviously, there’s an epidemic of men on podcasts, and so this is our effort to uproot that. Yeah, I just feel like if you just talk more than them, faster than them, outsmart them, and women are funnier than men, right? Exactly. PESSOA I think that we are two people who have opinions, but we also aren’t vicious people, unless you give us a reason to be. Then I can get creative. But we are friends in real life, and we also have different perspectives on reality TV , being as Ciara is on reality television, and I am addicted to it, because I am a child of Nick and Jessica, the Newlyweds . MILLER She’s an encyclopedia of pop culture and all things reality TV. Like, I actually think she has a photographic memory. PESSOA I wish. MILLER No, I think you do, though. PESSOA Thank you. But I think that we have a great chemistry, and I think we’re funny. MILLER Yeah, we got jokes. PESSOA We got jokes. MILLER I got a few jokes. PESSOA You know what it is? We bite into a bit. Oh, we love us a bit. MILLER You know what? Silly geese. PESSOA There’s not enough geese out there, and we come from a long line of silly geese. There’s a lot of people, I think, in the internet now that know that rage baiting gets a lot of views. And I’m never gonna judge anybody for how they pay their bills, but I think there’s not a lot of people online talking about what they do like, and I’m sick of hearing what people don’t like. So, I think that’s also an effort that we try to make, where I don’t want to ragebait anybody. I don’t want to talk about what I don’t like. Let’s talk about what we do like and still humanize people, but you can humanize somebody and poke fun and still hold them accountable, and I think we dance that dance pretty well. Hey, she’ll be on Dancing With the Stars later this year! MILLER Plugging my next project. PESSOA Period. Team Ciara. #TeamCiara, I’ll be starting that one. Tefi Pessoa and Ciara Miller on ‘Love Island USA Aftersun.’ What is the filming schedule like for Aftersun ? Aftersun comes out on Saturdays, but what does the week leading up to its release look like? MILLER A lot of studying, a lot of lying in the sun. PESSOA A lot of lying in the sun, and studying. MILLER A lot of catching up on episodes. I don’t let her talk to me about the episodes prior to. Because every single week, she will [try]. I’m like, we have to record a whole show on this, so don’t tell me your opinion on Tuesday, so save your opinions. PESSOA We show up on 10 a.m. Friday. MILLER We get in glam, and then when we sit down, that’s us discussing it for the first time. I take notes in my phone as I’m watching it. PESSOA And I don’t. MILLER Because she has photographic memory, and I don’t. PESSOA But we start at like 3:30 [p.m.], and then we usually sit down, because we we love our partners, Cuervo Tequila, and we do plugs for them, and then we wrap around 9 p.m., and then we don’t talk to each other in the car on the way home. MILLER We’ve said everything. PESSOA Truly, to the editor’s credit, we talk so fucking much, and still it’s like a seamless show. MILLER Which people were like, “Ciara, you’re talking over her.” It’s literally us talking so much that they have to figure out where to cut things, because we don’t shut up. PESSOA We don’t shut up. But I hate that too, that people are like, “Oh, you’re talking over each other” — you’ve never seen two girls excited to see each other at brunch? Shut up. Whatever. Also I know no one talks over me. Are you kidding? ( Laughs .) Who? Literally, are you crazy? What are you talking about? She’s not talking over me, I’m letting her get a word in. MILLER No, literally, I’m fighting for my life sometimes ( Laughs ). I wanted to put that on Threads one day. PESSOA No one’s talked over me since 1998. As each new season of Love Island comes out, fans have more and more to say. This year there’s been a conversation about fans believing that votes could have been rigged, specifically in the case of Sol and Gabriel’s dumpings. What are your opinions on fans believing that the voting or dumpings could have been rigged? PESSOA Well, I think Americans have been saying that every voting. There is something wrong with this electoral college. MILLER But, you know, no, I don’t think [votings are rigged.] It feels like very low stakes to rig the voting. But, who are we? PESSOA I think NBC would rig the voting so they can keep their $100k ( Laughs ), not give it away. Period. But, I don’t think that’s rigged at all. And I think production, it’s very fair, to be honest. There are some people that we don’t want to see go, and alas… MILLER If someone is rigging the election, I feel like B613 can figure it out. You both have experience navigating the whirlwind that is social media. As you meet with dumped islanders on Aftersun as they are preparing to enter post-villa life, is there any advice that you share with them, or would you rather not give them advice and let them navigate as they seem fit? PESSOA I always tell them don’t let people tell you who you are. MILLER And also it’s hard to give advice, because you really do have to go through it to kind of learn the ups, the downs of it. But I always say, keep your friends that you had from the very beginning. Like, they will keep you grounded, they will tell you everything you need to know, and the internet is not a real place. Although people have parasocial relationships with you, they don’t know you, so keep that in mind. PESSOA We’re also sometimes the first friendly faces that aren’t production that [dumped Islanders] see, and we remember that these kids are 24, 25… MILLER Yeah, they’re babies. PESSOA So, we do try to be like, “Hi, you’re safe here. We’re gonna talk, and we’re not going to make you out to be this, like, villain edit thing,” you know. MILLER But also, we’re not coming here with pitchforks, and their perspective is valid. Obviously, we can push and pull a little bit, but at the end of the day, they lived with that experience, we didn’t. PESSOA A microphone is dangerous enough; we don’t have to do anything. Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa on ‘Love Island USA’ season eight. Season eight is nearing its end. What couples or Islanders are y’all’s favorite at the moment, and is there a couple that you think will definitely make it to the finale? PESSOA Course, I do! MILLER Brinity! We are huge Brinity fans. I’m actually very much an Aniya and Carl fan. I hope that they can ride this out. PESSOA We want Aniya to be with somebody who wants her, and that she also wants, so if that’s not meant to be… MILLER Right, that’s true. I’m really interested to see what Melanie and Sincere are gonna do, you know, what’s gonna happen post-Movie Night. PESSOA I’ve been Melanie and Sincere in many scenarios. MILLER Oh my God. PESSOA And that’s being 25. The final part of Movie Night will come out tomorrow. Have you seen that cut yet? MILLER We actually haven’t. I just want to let you know, they send us the episodes after it’s aired in America. PESSOA Yeah, we just get a link. MILLER So, we’re honestly behind. PESSOA We’re always behind it, because we don’t have VPNs, so we’re always a little bit delayed. So when an episode comes out, I will not check Threads. MILLER Yeah, you can’t even get on Threads because the clips are everywhere. PESSOA I’m like, “Ciara, he came out of a cake?!” Are there any moments that were not shown in the first part of Movie Night that you think have to be shown in the second part on Thursday? PESSOA I feel like they’re all dangerous. MILLER KC has to have another section in there. Corbin has to have another section in there. KC and Sincere both need to have like two parters, but that’s my opinion. But we’ll see. We’re nearing the end of Love Island season eight, so what are both of y’all’s plans post- Love Island Aftersun ? Do you see reality TV both in your future? Ciara, we know you’re doing Dancing With the Stars. PESSOA I’m going to be front row at Dancing With the Stars with a shirt that says “Dance Ciara, Dance.” They’re gonna think it’s, like, a memorial shirt. ( Laughs .) I don’t think I have any reality TV aspirations. MILLER She’s gonna be marked safe. PESSOA I’m gonna be marked safe. But when I am in my 50s, I will be probably a Real Housewife in some city. So, like a couple years after Ciara. MILLER I’m just trying to get back to America. PESSOA She’s trying to get home back to Jasper, her cat. MILLER I obviously have Dancing With the Stars . I gotta get in the gym, because that was my goal for Fiji. PESSOA I would love to see you in, like, Special Forces . That would be fun. MILLER I’m not doing that. PESSOA But I think it’d be very fun. Not for me; I never want to do that, but I’d like to see you. Amora Robinson, Sydney Eugene, Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa on ‘Love Island USA Aftersun.’ *** Love Island Aftersun airs Saturdays on Peacock; New episodes of Love Island USA season eight, hosted by Ariana Madix, drop Thursdays through Tuesdays. Catch up on all of THR ‘s season eight coverage here . THR

title: Josh Brolin Almost Quit Ridley Scott’s ‘The Dog Stars’ After First Day: “I’ve Got To Get The F*ck Out Of Here”
full_text: Skip to main content By Glenn Garner Glenn Garner Associate Editor @xoxo_glenn More Stories By Glenn Kathy Griffin Thinks She’s “Banned” From ‘The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon’ Weird Al Yankovic Turned Down “Nice Pile Of Money” For AI Ad: “I Can’t Be The Poster Boy For AI” Josh Brolin Almost Quit Ridley Scott’s ‘The Dog Stars’ After First Day: “I’ve Got To Get The F*ck Out Of Here” View All July 1, 2026 6:20pm Josh Brolin in 'The Dog Stars' 20th Century Studios With his latest role in The Dog Stars , Josh Brolin was ready to throw in the towel on the first day. The Oscar nominee recently explained why director Ridley Scott “bugged me out” and nearly made him drop out of his role as ex-Marine Bangley in the post-apocalyptic drama, which premieres Aug. 28. “Ridley was talking a lot of stories and not really rehearsing, and it bugged me out, and I got really scared,” he told Empire. “I went back, called my agent and said, ‘I want out. Something’s really wrong, and I’ve got to get the f*ck out of here.'” Related Stories News ‘The Dog Stars’: First Look At Ridley Scott’s Post-Apocalyptic Thriller – CinemaCon Casting Margaret Qualley, Josh Brolin And Guy Pearce Join Jacob Elordi In Ridley Scott’s Next Film ‘The Dog Stars’ Brolin added, “Luckily my agent is a close friend and he said, ‘Rest for a day.’ I was like, ‘No, man, I know what the f*ck you’re doing. It’s not one of those day-things.’ And I was right.” Watch on Deadline Despite previously working with Scott on American Gangster (2007), Brolin was not accustomed to the director’s new high-energy, multi-camera style used on The Dog Stars . But after seeing the dailies, the actor began to understand Scott’s vision. Jacob Elordi and Josh Brolin in ‘The Dog Stars’ (2026) (Fabio Lovino/20th Century Studios/Courtesy Everett Collection) “[Ridley] goes, ‘Come here,’ and he brought me into his trailer, and played the scene we had just finished,” recalled Brolin. “It was a really good, very dynamic scene between me and Jacob, and he goes, ‘Okay?’ I go, ‘Okay,’ and then I started to feed off that.” Based on the novel by Peter Heller, Jacob Elordi stars in The Dog Stars as a grief-stricken pilot who’s accompanied by his loyal dog in navigating a post-pandemic American wilderness, clinging to survival and the faint hope of human connection after a mysterious radio transmission suggests they may not be alone. Penned by Mark L. Smith, the veteran scribe behind Alejandro G. Iñárritu’s The Revenant , the film also stars Margaret Qualley, Guy Pearce and Benedict Wong. Scott produced the picture alongside Scott Free president Michael Pruss, as well as Smith and Cliff Roberts. ‘Minions & Monsters’ Opening Day Heading To $13M+ Domestic ‘The Murder of JonBenét Ramsey’ Limited Series Lands At Netflix; Release Window Set ‘Project Hail Mary’ Landing On Prime Video After 105-Day Theatrical Window Michael Weatherly Returning To CBS Drama For Season 24 After Spinoff’s Axing Read More About: Comments

title: ‘Love Island USA Aftersun’s Ciara Miller & Tefi Pessoa On Humanizing Islanders, Pitch To Host Reunion & Not Being Afraid Of Sharing Opinions
full_text: Skip to main content By Armando Tinoco Armando Tinoco Staff Writer @armietinoco More Stories By Armando Morgan Kohan On ‘Sullivan’s Crossing’ Without Scott Patterson’s Sully: “It Shakes Things Up In A Nice Way” Liz McGraw Fires Back At Speculation Over Quitting ‘RHORI’ With Claims Of “Skeletons” & “Jail”: “I Have Never Been Investigated” Sadie Sink On Working With Tom Holland In ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’: “He Was Just So Relaxed And Open” View All July 1, 2026 6:00pm Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa of 'Love Island USA Aftersun' Peacock Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa , the two new bombshells who entered the Love Island USA villa to revamp Aftersun . The weekly talk show features Miller and Pessoa giving a debrief of the Peacock dating reality series, as well as exclusive never-before-seen footage and interviews with dumped Islanders. The change has paid off for the streamer, as the companion series saw a 144% increase in audience from its Season 7 debut, with viewers curious to see the new format. Miller and Pessoa are a perfect match, have great chemistry on camera, and bounce off of each other with such ease. The co-hosts knew each other beforehand and met while supporting Kamala Harris’s 2024 presidential campaign. Watch on Deadline Related Stories News Liz McGraw Fires Back At Speculation Over Quitting 'RHORI' With Claims Of "Skeletons" & "Jail": "I Have Never Been Investigated" News Liz McGraw Exits 'The Real Housewives Of Rhode Island' After One Season “I think once you meet someone who has similar core values and music taste,” Pessoa told Deadline in an interview while proceeding to sing alongside Miller to Kandi Burruss’s “Don’t Think I’m Not.” “We just have a lot of things in common.” Miller added, “She’s a Cancer, I’m a Capricorn, so we’re not exactly the same. So it’s just the same level of weird. We love to LARP [live action role play], we love a bit … it kind of just works.” The Summer House star notes that they also work so well because they bring two different perspectives: Miller brings the reality TV side, while Pessoa is “an encyclopedia for pop culture,” adding, “I think it’s a good mix of knowledge.” Pessoa and Miller’s conversation about Love Island USA is good vibes, and they’re not about tearing each other apart over their different takes. “When we have a different opinion, we don’t discount it,” Pessoa says. “We’re here to gab, and we’re here to giggle, and not enough people are giggling on TV anymore.” Although the duo does make viewers giggle, the passionate fanbase is not always giggling with them, as they have recently experienced. Both hosts pushed back on criticism from viewers when they found themselves on the other side of the debate in the KC and Aniya recoupling drama following Casa Amor. Tefi Pessoa and Ciara Miller hosting ‘Love Island USA Aftersun’ Peacock “It’s scary, and sometimes it’s not a safe environment, especially on Threads,” Miller says. “But you know what, everyone’s entitled to their opinion. We’re throwing different ideas out. I’m offering the perspective of being on reality TV, knowing being subject to editing. I know there’s so many different things at play.” Miller added that her “opinions aren’t concrete” and “they are going to change the further we get into the series. You know things are happening, and things change every day with Love Island . I’m never giving an opinion with a standpoint [that] it’s going to stay there forever because that’s just not realistic.” Pessoa offered the perspective that when some viewers see someone on TV, “they feel like that person isn’t a real person” and there’s a “lack of seeing someone’s humanity,” adding, “If you’re going to disagree with me, at least disagree with me for my honest and real opinion and maybe their opinion of me will evolve as Love Island evolves.” One thing for sure is that Miller is not afraid of sharing her opinion as “this is what we’re here to do,” adding, “We are in the business of humanizing people on reality TV. It is a pressure cooker in there. I wouldn’t have signed up for this job if I didn’t want to see the perspective of everything, even though I might not agree with everyone — especially the boys, but I have to try and see it.” Ciara Miller and Tefi Pessoa hosting ‘Love Island USA Aftersun’ Peacock Miller also pitched returning to the villa alongside Pessoa to host another game and said, “We’re also down to host the reunion as well.” Pessoa jumped in, adding, “Yeah, we’d love to host a reunion.” “We’re putting out our bids,” Miller said, with Pessoa jokingly saying, “Andy, go home!” Miller added that they both come “a lot cheaper than Andy Cohen” to host the reunion. But ultimately, she said, “As long as they put us in the audience, we will be happy.” “I’m happy to sit in the audience too,” Pessoa seconded. Love Island USA Aftersun streams Saturdays at 9 p.m. ET and 6 p.m. PT on Peacock. ‘Minions & Monsters’ Opening Day Heading To $13M+ Domestic ‘The Murder of JonBenét Ramsey’ Limited Series Lands At Netflix; Release Window Set ‘Project Hail Mary’ Landing On Prime Video After 105-Day Theatrical Window Michael Weatherly Returning To CBS Drama For Season 24 After Spinoff’s Axing Read More About: Comments

title: Rosalía’s Brilliant ‘Lux’ Tour Brings Cross-Disciplinary Delight and Splendor to L.A.’s Kia Forum: Concert Review
full_text: Jul 1, 2026 5:53pm PT Rosalía’s Brilliant ‘Lux’ Tour Brings Cross-Disciplinary Delight and Splendor to L.A.’s Kia Forum: Concert Review A confessional cameo from fellow Latin music superstar Karol G was just one highlight among many in a night that served to prove the Spanish-bred star is one of our most ambitious and best entertainers. By Chris Willman Plus Icon Chris Willman Senior Music Writer and Chief Music Critic ChrisWillman Latest Rosalía’s Brilliant ‘Lux’ Tour Brings Cross-Disciplinary Delight and Splendor to L.A.’s Kia Forum: Concert Review 10 hours ago Jury Says Chris Brown Owes Housekeeper $13 Million for Disfigurement in Dog Mauling Attack 1 day ago Vanilla Ice’s Freedom 250 Concert in D.C. Canceled Due to ‘Inclement Weather’ That Doesn’t Materialize 5 days ago See All Beth Savaro Do you want to know how good Rosalía ‘s Monday night show at the Kia Forum was? Here is how good Rosalía’s Monday night show at the Forum was… Midway through the set, as is the custom on her “Lux” tour, the Spanish singer-songwriter took time out to have a candid dialogue with a guest superstar — each settling down in adjoining, enclosed boxes, set up like a Catholic confessional space that just happens to have a live camera feed, with the purpose of spilling tea on a bad romance. Here, that surprise confessor was Karol G , who’s been perceived by some fans as caught up in a rivalry with Rosalía, although there was no sign of any beef here. Karol spilled the beans on an unnamed ex-boyfriend most of the audience took to be Feid, talking about his mysterious reluctance to have her accompany him on his birthdays, and how the fourth year that this happened, it was the straw that broke the camel’s back and broke them up. Related Stories British Government Likely to Intervene in Paramount's Proposed $110 Billion Takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Warner Bros. Acquires Tahereh Mafi’s Bestselling Dystopian YA Series 'Shatter Me' (EXCLUSIVE) This was a deeply juicy moment (if you speak Spanish, anyway, or had someone nearby to translate): Here were two of music’s biggest stars, in sudden, public alignment, at last, with the bonus of some deep dish being shared about a third fellow celebrity. Who could ask for anything more out of a concert? Popular on Variety Except for, you know, music. So here’s the proof of how good Rosalía’s Monday night show at the Forum was: As much spicy fun as that Karol G encounter was, it was only about the 10th or 15th best moment in the show. The “confessional” segment makes for a hell of a conversation piece, but there’s a lot of heavenliness in this tour’s weirdly wieldy mixture of pop art, high art, diva deliciousness and spiritual ardor. Expectations for this summer’s “Lux” tour were high after her album of that name turned up on half the world’s top 10 lists last December. Two weeks into the U.S. leg, those hopes are being met, and then some. We’ve seen some awfully good concerts come through Los Angeles recently (Raye’s, Lily Allen’s), but Rosalía’s brilliant road show is up there in the running for Tour of the Year honors. Pound for pound, it offers as much ambition as the “Lux” album itself, a project that had critics and fans striving to find adjectives for pretentious-in-a-good-way. The “Lux” tour comes cloaked in a certain amount of literal and figurative religious garb, per the record. But there are many more moments in which Rosalía comes off as either a down-to-earth pop girlie or as one of the great young song-and-dance entertainers of our time. With or without the album’s deistic references carrying over into the stage show, it’s divine. Rosalía at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, California, June 29, 2026 Beth Savaro Rosalía’s show is delightfully cross-disciplinary, bringing in aspects of ballet and classical music. But at the very beginning of all these cross-referencing moments, there is an actual cross. That occurs right at the start, when some workers bring out a large, upright, white crate, marked “Fragile”… the contents of which are, of course, Rosalía, standing on a small pedestal in a tutu. When the box she’s been carted in in is fully unfolded, the lid attachment ensures that it is laid out in the shape of a cross. Fortunately, the Christian symbolism isn’t too heavy-handed from there. The show is going to be way too visually busy to stay that sectarian, when Rosalía has plans to evoke everything from flamenco culture to Bob Fosse over the ensuing 110 minutes. The opening stretch is the show’s most static, although there’s not a dull moment in it, as Rosalía performs the first five songs from the mostly stately “Lux,” almost in order. Her entrance has been preceded by an overture performed by an orchestra of about 20 pieces, placed in the middle of the arena’s floor. The neo-classical nature of this first “Lux” section is augmented by occasional blasts of deep, electronic bass sounds. Those thrilling Sensurround effects might make you fear that vibrations frpom the low, low, low rumbling might interfere with planes passing over the Forum to land at LAX, but that is somebody else’s problem. Rosalía at the Kia Forum, June 29, 2026 Chris Willman/Variety The upshot of this first section is that while other pop stars have been partying in Ibiza, Rosalía has been taking lessons. Lessons in standing and dancing on pointe, and lessons in being able to operatically deliver what credibly sounds like an aria, in her Italian-language original, “Mio Cristo Piange Diamante.” She could coolly pretend that she’s always been this accomplished in the fine arts, but Rosalía took an early time out to thank two people in the audience, a choreographer she’s worked with for eight years, Charm La’Donna, and a voice teacher she’s had for 10, Eric Vetro, while noting, “I only had one month and a half to learn how to dance in pointe shoes for the first time in my life, and it was one of the scariest things I ever did, I’ve ever tried in my life. But I know it’s gonna be a lifelong process. I’ve accepted it, just as it is with my voice.” These acknowledgements of the challenges she’s faced in learning these classical traditions immediately took the piss out of any pretentiousness she might face for having had the nerve to adopt them. The show did not stay strictly in the realm of the philharmonic much past the half-hour mark, although, thankfully, the orchestra itself was around for the long haul. A natural transition point arrived with “Berghain,” the first single from “Lux,” which starts off as a symphony and ends with a Bjork lament and profane Mike Tyson sample (both reproduced for the stage version). “His rage is my rage,” she sang, as the electronica took over in the second half of the song, and the orchestra pit turned into a rave, with the string players waving their bows in the air like they just didn’t care. Rosalía had just gone through her first costume change, exchanging the all-white garb of the novitiate for a sleek black dress, and she affirmed the mood shift with a call to action: “You didn’t come to this show just to cry. You came to shake some ass,” she insisted — probably not being too presumptuous on behalf of an audience that had been reared on her more conventional earlier albums. The singer reverted to a more clearly pop-based “Motomami” form for the next stretch… until it was time to cover Frankie Valli. Cover Frankie Valli? That’s right: Her rendition of “Can’t Take My Eyes Off of You” had Rosalía positioning herself as a painting in an art museum, gazed upon from behind a rope-chain by a couple dozen fans who’d been selected to come on stage and portray gallery-goers. Before anyone had too long a time to ponder what statement was being made here about the artist/subject gaze, it was on to more provocative stuff. Prior to the next number, “La Perla,” her takedown of a not-so-swell guy, she led into that with the confessional segment with Karol G, turning the remove of the museum into a church where no secrets are kept. If what’s good for the goose is also good for the gander, then maybe Rosalía should confess something about her own love life, on the last night of the tour, if not sooner. But for the time being, everyone got a kick out of Karol taking a spot that previously had been taken on tour by guests from Marcello Hernandez to Maggie Rogers (whose MSG tale of dating an alleged cheater who wrote for the New York Times set off quite a journalistic witch hunt in mid-June, but that’s another story). “Look, if there’s anything you’ve been wanting to get off your chest, something where you just think, ‘This is a safe space for me’…” Rosalía said in Spanish, as opposed to the English she spoke in for most of the evening. Karol was well-prepared for the drill, explaining how her significant other became oddly avoidant around his birthdays, even though “who wouldn’t want to celebrate their birthday with their partner?” She was “waiting for the excuse for the next year to not be there for the birthday, and then the next year to not be there, and the next year to not be there… Every year was like that, right? There was always a fucking excuse. … What’s the prayer I’m supposed to say, to sort this out?” For the fourth and final birthday, a trip was planned, and at the airport, “he tells me, ‘Take these suitcases down.’ I just stood there. No way, you jerk. But I got off that flight — and I mean, I really got off it… We didn’t celebrate it or anything else.” Rosalía tried drawing out a few more details than Karol seemed ready to go into, before putting a punchline on it, saying (again, in Spanish): “Girl, I have to say that in my country, when someone is a real jerk —a piece of work — we say, ‘What a gem that one is.’ We’re going to celebrate bigger and better than any birthday ever. Because you got rid of that son-of-a-bitch ‘gem.'” For everyone on hand for night 1 at the Forum, it felt like being present at the formation of the Latin Superstar Diva Avengers. Or so we could hope. That was not the end of the night’s conversation, as, during a balladic segment where Rosalía laid atop a piano, sipping champagne with her accompanist in advance of “Sauvignon Blanc,” she ended up in a back-and-forth with a birthday boy in the audience named Adrian who turned out to be an instructor who said he teaches a “master class” on the album “Lux.” The album is rich enough that this wouldn’t strike anyone as difficult to believe; the only question is whether he’ll be able to add an additional summer school adjunct course just about this tour. Rosalía at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, California, June 29, 2026 Beth Savaro If the “Lux” tour should end up as coursework of some sort, the most class time might be spent on Rosalía’s performance of “La Perla,” which has choreography already familiar to millions of Americans through shared video clips. In a gambit that Bob Fosse could be proud of, the singer’s troupe of dancers dons all-black, so as to disappear into the woodwork, except for their long white gloves. As the one number in the show choreographed by the Greek master Dimitris Papaioannou, the “La Perla” routine creates masterful illusions in which those seemingly detached forearms come together to form shapes around the singer — a halo, a veil, a frame, even what looks like a metronome as the whole ensemble tilts in one direction. It’s worth the price of admission. That is not to undervalue the rest of the dazzling choreography that takes place over the course of the night, most of it designed by the trio (La)Horde, who are part of the Ballet National de Marseilles, and the aforementioned Charm La’Donna. None of these dances are very alike, and few of them are ostensibly very over-exuberant or showy, but they all contribute to a vibe that feels in turn sly, soul-searching or just friendly. Rosalía is so into God in these songs, or at least the idea of God as a concept filtered through his female saints, that you aren’t necessarily prepared for her to go sexy on us. And she doesn’t, a lot. But in “Saoko,” the camera goes in for a closeup of a brief bit of twerking as the singer, clad in what looks like a high-fashion designer’s idea of a black widow’s dress, turns out to be wearing hot pink shorts underneath. At the very end of the show, in “Focu ’Ranni,” she and the dancers are all wearing angel wings, in keeping with the night’s recurring spiritual themes, and yet the hoofers are also wearing the equivalent of mom jeans. That exemplifies how there is a sort of coziness to this show, and to Rosalía’s very presence, no matter how ornate and lofty it gets. Another clear highlight was “La Rumba del Perdón,” which found Rosalía finally visiting the orchestra in the middle of the arena, and treating it as a B-stage. She spoke of her love of flamenco and, while not doing a formal dance for that segment, kicked up her heels nonetheless in an irresistible violet dress. On her way out to that remote spot, she sang “Dios Es Un Stalker” (“God Is a Stalker”) while strolling through a fenced-off passageway through the middle of the auditorium, occasionally putting the microphone into the face of a fervent fan. A performer sometimes gets lucky with crowd interaction moments like that and sometimes doesn’t. But few of us will soon forget the sight of the guy dressed as a nun, screaming the lyrics into a mic that Rosalía admirably did not pull away, apparently enjoying his joyful catharsis as much as the rest of us. Rosalía at the Kia Forum, June 29, 2026 Chris Willman/Variety At the very end of the show, in “Focu ’Ranni,” she and the dancers are all wearing angel wings, in keeping with the night’s recurring spiritual themes. Yet these heavenly hoofers were also wearing what looked like mom jeans, and indulging in a light-hearted pillow fight. That exemplifies how there is a sort of coziness to this show, and to Rosalía’s surprisingly unpretentious presence, no matter how ornate and lofty it gets. There are a lot of images or sounds at cross-purposes on the “Lux” tour, and always delightfully so. I think of the moment when a strobe-light contraption is lowered over the orchestra during a particularly dance-oriented piece of music… and then it starts swinging back and forth, emitting smoke, having turned from a light fixture into what liturgical churchgoers will recognize as an incense-dispensing thurible. Like that massive light fixture turned holy smoke machine, this is one giant Transformer of a show. And if you have a love for classical traditions and everything a modern arena pop show is expected to offer, you may feel like you’ve landed somewhere on the heaven-and-earth continuum, too.
